commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The protected maintainer label and remaining real-proof/message-delivery contract judgment require maintainer handling rather than an automated repair branch.; Cleared: The diff only changes agent delivery control flow and a focused Vitest regression; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, credentials, packaging, or external code execution. (sha=f667786ed9a7199217cbe184f5c96603c5819c8a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch deletes the `completion_handoff_pending` failure return for pending plain subagent completion direct announces and adds a focused regression test for the accepted-response path.

PR surface: Source -12, Tests +38. Total +26 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main maps the pending plain completion handoff to a failed direct delivery, and registry code records false delivery as retry/give-up state. I did not run a live current-main gateway/subagent reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected `maintainer` label and remaining real-proof/message-delivery contract judgment require maintainer handling rather than an automated repair branch.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve pending subagent completion announces
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93323 by treating non-terminal direct announce responses as accepted delivery for subagent completion handoffs.

Previously `subagent-announce-delivery` mapped `accepted` / `started` / `in_flight` gateway responses to `delivered: false` for the plain completion-message branch, which let the registry retry budget exhaust and abandon the announce. The fix removes that inconsistent special case so pending direct announces return `delivered: true` consistently with the existing media completion path.

## Root cause

`sendSubagentAnnounceDirectly` already classifies gateway `accepted`, `started`, and `in_flight` as non-terminal pending states. For completion handoffs with no media and no required message-tool delivery, it nevertheless returned `completion_handoff_pending` as a failed delivery. That turned a healthy accepted gateway run into a retryable/final failure at the registry layer.

## Changes

- Remove the `completion_handoff_pending` failure branch for non-terminal direct announce responses.
- Add a regression test for pending plain completion announce delivery without media fallback.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Subagent completion announce delivery no longer treats a non-terminal gateway response as a failed delivery when there are no media URLs and no message-tool delivery requirement.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, lease `tbx_01kve06jgaepzt3h2earbapzpp` (`harbor-shrimp`), GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27781148636. The run synced the final rebased PR head `f667786ed9a7199217cbe184f5c96603c5819c8a` as a delegated overlay from `origin/main` `317919ec529ed8b32b970e53d4548c8590cef3ad`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- bash -lc 'OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed && corepack pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose'
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
Changes synced in 4083ms (2 file(s), targeted)
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts: core production

[check:changed] typecheck core
[check:changed] typecheck core tests
[check:changed] lint core changed files
[check:changed] runtime import cycles
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       101 passed (101)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.16s

blacksmith run summary sync=delegated command=5m25.12s total=5m36.729s exit=0
{"provider":"blacksmith-testbox","leaseId":"tbx_01kve06jgaepzt3h2earbapzpp","slug":"harbor-shrimp","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":0}
```

Observed result after fix: The changed gate selected the core production file and its core test lane, then passed typecheck, changed-file lint, import-cycle guard, and the full `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` regression file. The new regression `preserves pending completion announce delivery without media fallback` passed, proving a gateway `accepted` response now reports `delivered: true` and does not trigger direct fallback delivery for the plain completion-handoff branch.

What was not tested: I did not run a full live gateway/subagent workload under event-loop pressure, and I did not claim this closes the broader delivery-loss trackers in #44925 or #52249. The proof targets the implicated delivery decision and adjacent completion-delivery regression suite.

## Additional verification

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed locally after final rebase

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
# passed locally after final rebase
```

## Notes

No agent transcript is included in this PR body.
target.author: sallyom

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
